title: Xarcce Huwla
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}} {{Character |type=New Republic |image=[[File:XarrceHuwla-XW21.png]] |name=Xarcce Huwla |homeworld=[[Jiroch-Saloch]]{{InsiderCite|59|Who's Who in Rogue Squadron}} |birth= |death= |species=[[Tunroth]] |gender= |pronouns=She/her |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes=[[Color/Legends|Black]][[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 21|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 21]] |skin=Yellow |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=*[[Alliance of Free Planets]][[X-Wing: Rogue Leader 3|''X-Wing: Rogue Leader'' 3]] *[[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] **[[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|With you around, Xarcce, who needs Plourr?|Ibtisam praises Xarcce Huwla's combat prowess|[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 30|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 30]]}} '''Xarcce Huwla''' was a [[Tunroth]] who volunteered to join the [[Alliance of Free Planets]]' [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] following the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]. Around nine [[Standard month/Legends|months]] after the battle, Huwla was recruited into the unit, by then under the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], but requested to be transferred out due to the [[Squadron/Legends|squadron's]] high number of [[Death/Legends|casualties]]. Unable to be moved immediately, her first deployment was the [[Battle of Brentaal IV (Galactic Civil War)|Battle of Brentaal IV]], where she and the squadron engaged in [[Starfighter combat/Legends|combat]] with the [[181st Imperial Fighter Wing/Legends|181st Imperial Fighter Wing]] at a [[Brentaal Moonbase|base]] on one of the [[Brentaal IV/Legends|planet's]] [[Moon/Legends|moons]]. As part of the Brentaal IV campaign, Huwla also participated in battles on [[Vuultin]] and [[Oradin]], [[City/Legends|cities]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]]. At Vuultin, the squadron supported [[Aggressor Wing]] and encountered the 181st once more, successfully forcing more 181st [[Starfighter/Legends|fighters]] at Oradin to leave and allowing the New Republic to capture it in the process. At Oradin, Huwla and the squadron shot down additional [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] fighters, including those from the 181st. By the [[Time/Legends|time]] the campaign had ended, Huwla managed to score impressive kills against the famous Imperial unit and found friends within Rogue Squadron, never speaking of transfers again. Huwla and Rogue Squadron were later deployed to the planet [[Axxila/Legends|Axxila]] on an [[Undercover operation on Axxila|undercover mission]], where she engaged [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] in flight as well as in [[Unarmed combat/Legends|unarmed combat]] at an establishment alongside fellow Rogues [[Nrin Vakil]] and [[Ibtisam]]. Around [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]], the squadron was called up for a mission to [[Evacuation of Ciutric|rescue]] [[Grand Vizier/Legends|Grand Vizier]] [[Sate Pestage/Legends|Sate Pestage]] from the world [[Ciutric IV]]. During the mission, Rogue Squadron and Aggressor Wing supported [[Commando Team One]]'s extraction of Pestage but were forced to retreat to the world's surface due to the arrival of two [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]] in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]]. Despite failing to rescue Pestage, they successfully defeated the [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial forces]] and forced the Star Destroyers to flee. ==Biography== ===Becoming a Rogue=== {{Quote|…I am honored to be here, but I request immediate transfer.|Huwla, to Wedge Antilles regarding transfer from Rogue Squadron|}} Xarcce Huwla was a [[Tunroth]] from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]]''[[Ultimate Alien Anthology]]'' [[Jiroch-Saloch]]. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] against the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], in the [[Post-Imperial era/Legends|aftermath]] of the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]][[X-Wing: Rogue Leader 3|''X-Wing: Rogue Leader'' 3]] of [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]]''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' and following the [[Mission to Tralus|capture]] of Imperial [[General/Legends|General]] [[Weir/Legends|Weir]], Huwla was one of several volunteer [[Rebel pilot/Legends|pilots]] for the [[Alliance of Free Planets]]' [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]], a famous [[X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing starfighter]] unit. The volunteers were stationed aboard a [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]], where they were briefed by [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]], who stated that the [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] was tasked with the most dangerous assignments. [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]], the newly appointed [[Rogue Leader/Legends|leader]] of the squadron, then entered the briefing deck and ordered the [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] to enter their [[Starship/Legends|ships]] before they departed the [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]]. [[File:Recruits.jpg|thumb|left|350px|Huwla (fourth from left) and three other new recruits report in for duty.]] Prior to joining Rogue Squadron, Huwla, a longtime veteran,[[Star Wars Handbook 1: X-Wing Rogue Squadron|''Star Wars Handbook'' 1: ''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'']] had completed twenty-three combat missions and was aware of the squadron's history, such as the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battles of Hoth]] and [[Endor/Legends|Endor]], recent adventures like the [[Mission to Mrlsst|battle]] at the planet [[Mrlsst/Legends|Mrlsst]],[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 8|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 8]] and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance's]] victory at the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. Around nine [[Standard month/Legends|months]] after the Battle of Endor,''[[Star Wars Omnibus: X-Wing Rogue Squadron Volume 3]]'' Huwla was one of four new recruits, which also included [[Standro Jcir]], [[Avan Beruss]], and [[Dar Keyis]], for Rogue Squadron, by then operating under the successor to the Alliance, the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], stationed at [[Base Delta-Nine]]. They were consulted by Antilles and fellow squadron member [[Tycho Celchu/Legends|Tycho Celchu]], the former viewing a [[Datapad/Legends|datapad]] displaying the profiles of Huwla and the other recruits. After Antilles dismissed the recruits, Huwla requested to speak with him and Celchu privately. The Tunroth's first request was to be transferred out of the squadron immediately, worried about the New Republic's habit of sending the unit to impossible situations and stating that she wished not to die as a Rogue as the chances of [[Death/Legends|death]] were much higher; the squadron had a high death count. Antilles, however, was unable to move her out immediately.{{CSWECite|II|69|Huwla, Xarrce}} After he dismissed her, she met with [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Wes Janson/Legends|Wes Janson]] and was billeted in fellow [[New Republic pilot/Legends|pilot]] [[Feylis Ardele]]'s quarters. ===Battle of Brentaal IV=== ====Meeting the 181st==== {{Quote|Tycho speaks of fear to relieve it. Plourr speaks against fear to hide it.''"
"''I am no coward.''"
"''Ah, then you are fool, since only fool does not wish to know lethal enemy.''"
"''I am no fool, either.|Huwla and Plourr Ilo discuss their upcoming fight with Fel and the 181st|}} On her first deployment, Huwla joined Rogue Squadron in supporting [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Horton Salm/Legends|Horton Salm's]] [[Aggressor Wing]] in their [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|fighters]] in an [[Battle of Brentaal IV (Galactic Civil War)|attack]] on an Imperial [[Brentaal Moonbase|base]] on a [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of the world [[Brentaal IV/Legends|Brentaal IV]], where they [[Starfighter combat/Legends|fought]] with the [[181st Imperial Fighter Wing/Legends|181st Imperial Fighter Wing]] led by [[Baron of the Empire/Legends|Baron]] [[Soontir Fel/Legends|Soontir Fel]]. In the battle, Huwla shot down a 181st [[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|TIE interceptor]] heading towards her. The New Republic successfully captured the base, after which Rogue Squadron returned to Base Delta-Nine. There, Huwla played a [[Holotable/Legends|holotable]] [[Hologame/Legends|game]] with Keyis before Antilles arrived and changed the [[Hologram/Legends|hologram]] projection to that of the 181st [[TIE series/Legends|TIE]] [[Interceptor/Legends|interceptors]] and identified them as such. When Celchu pointed out that the 181st was Fel's unit and that the Baron was the best [[TIE pilot|Imperial pilot]] alive, fellow pilot [[Isplourrdacartha Estillo|Plourr Ilo]] stated they could "change that," to which Huwla responded they could not do so before "joining" pilots [[Derek Klivian/Legends|Derek Klivian]], who was shot down in the battle over Brentaal IV, and [[Biggs Darklighter/Legends|Biggs Darklighter]], who was killed in the Battle of Yavin. [[File:BattleOfBrentaalIV.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Huwla participated in the Battle of Brentaal IV.]] Huwla later participated in another holotable game, this [[Time/Legends|time]] with [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] Rogue [[Ibtisam]]. By that point, she had heard a rumor that[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 22|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 22]] the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Sith Lord]]''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] had his [[Darth Vader's TIE Advanced/Legends|personal starship]] created to prevent Fel from killing him in battle, which she pointed out to the squadron while they discussed a future engagement with Fel and the 181st. She proceeded to state that while Celchu spoke of fear to relieve it, Ilo was hiding her fear. Huwla called her a fool when Ilo stated she was not a coward and explained that only a fool did not wish to learn about a lethal enemy. Huwla and the squadron later donned their [[Rebel flight suit|flight suits]] and attended a briefing at the Brentaal IV moonbase. The unit was tasked with feinting a strike on [[Vuultin]], a [[City/Legends|city]] on the planet, and encountering the 181st to prevent them from reaching the New Republic assault [[Trooper/Legends|troops]] hitting another city, [[Oradin]]. At Vuultin, they engaged in a dogfight with the 181st as planned. Although Rogue Squadron was later ordered to return to the [[MC80A Home One Type Heavy Star Cruiser/Legends|Star Cruiser]] [[Independence/Legends|''Independence'']], the feint forced Imperial Admiral [[Lon Isoto]] to [[Commanding officer/Legends|command]] the 181st pilots at Oradin, including Fel, to leave the city and reinforce Vuultin instead, opening Oradin up for capture by the New Republic. ====Attack on Oradin==== {{Quote|I was cruising in on that squint and just got tunnel vision.''"
"''Dangerous doing so. I was pleased to shoot other squint orienting you.|Derek Klivian and Huwla discuss their performance at the Battle of Brentaal IV|[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 24|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 24]]}} Later, at Oradin, Huwla and Klivian inspected a [[Unidentified merchant (Oradin)|merchant's]] pots and cups, picking up and admiring [[Unidentified merchant's heirloom|one]] of them. When Klivian wished to purchase it, the [[Merchant/Legends|merchant]] claimed it was a very old heirloom and would not give it for less than one thousand [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]]. Huwla noticed that the [[Ultraviolet/Legends|ultraviolet]] reflectors resembled those made on[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 23|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 23]] the planet [[Sullust/Legends|Sullust]] in the past [[Standard decade/Legends|decade]], thus identifying the cup as a fake. The pair then left the merchant despite the latter wanting to bargain with them. Rogue Squadron was later briefed on another attack on Oradin, this time to cover Aggressor Wing's [[BTL-S3 Y-wing starfighter/Legends|Y-wings]] while they disabled ships that carried wealthy Imperial citizens fleeing the city. [[File:X-wing_24_07.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Huwla shot down multiple 181st fighters in the Brentaal IV campaign.]] The squadron shot down multiple Imperial [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighters]] over Oradin before the 181st arrived and engaged the Rogues once more. Huwla herself shot down a TIE interceptor targeting Klivian. By the end of the Battle of Brentaal IV, Fel was captured following a dogfight with Antilles, and [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial forces]] withdrew from Brentaal IV. At the [[MC80 Star Cruiser/Legends|MC80 Star Cruiser]] [[Home One/Legends|''Home One'']], the Rogues discussed their victory, with Huwla stating to Klivian that she was pleased to down the interceptor targeting him. With the campaign's end, Huwla had racked up multiple kills against the 181st. She had also befriended members of the squadron, later withdrew her transfer request, and ceased talk of transfers out of the unit completely. Around ten months after the Battle of Endor, Huwla, Rogue Squadron, and other New Republic personnel attended a [[Funeral of Rebel and New Republic pilots|funeral]] held aboard a [[Star Cruiser/Legends|Star Cruiser's]] deck for the Rebel and New Republic pilots lost at Brentaal IV and previous battles. Following the [[Funeral/Legends|funeral]], at a briefing room, when Antilles announced that Fel would be joining Rogue Squadron's ranks, [[Quarren/Legends|Quarren]] Rogue [[Nrin Vakil]] attempted to rush towards the former Imperial pilot in a state of anger, but Huwla and Ilo held him back until Fel exited the room.[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 26|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 26]] ===Action on Axxila=== {{Quote|Such trouble you find when I let you run off!''"
"''Xarcce, you call this trouble?|Huwla and Ibtisam, while fighting off pirates|[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 30|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 30]]}} Around eleven months after the Battle of Endor, Huwla and Rogue Squadron [[Raid on pirates|raided]] a group of [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] in an area of [[Realspace/Legends|space]]. The squadron shot down multiple [[TYE-wing|fighters]] before Antilles ordered that they allow them to escape.[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 28|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 28]] In an [[Undercover operation on Axxila|undercover operation]] on[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 29|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 29]] the planet{{CSWECite|I|50|Axxila}} [[Axxila/Legends|Axxila]], the New Republic's [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] and [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] met with [[Grand Vizier/Legends|Grand Vizier]] [[Sate Pestage]], who offered to leave the Imperial [[Capital/Legends|capital]] planet of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] undefended from a New Republic attack in exchange for a few worlds of his own. The news was later discussed by Huwla, Vakil, and Ibtisam at a building with Organa, Solo, and other Rogues who had just arrived there. [[FIle:HuwlaIbtisamVakilVsPirates-XWRS30.png|thumb|right|350px|Huwla (second from left), Ibtisam (third from left), and Vakil (top) fend off pirates.]] The trio later sat at an establishment on Axxila and had drinks, with Huwla drinking a [[lomin ale]]. They then discussed a group of pirates seated at another table, with Huwla complimenting Vakil's deduction that the group consisted of pilots. She then revealed to Ibtisam that Vakil used to worry about the Mon Calamari while the latter was on the ground; they had become close friends by that point. The pirate group began leaving the establishment just as Vakil ordered the two to report back to Antilles. However, Ibtisam decided to join Vakil in tailing the pirates. Having pointed out that that was not the original plan, Huwla herself confronted the pirates as a [[Unarmed combat/Legends|fight]] broke out between them and the three Rogues. The trio successfully incapacitated the pirates before they departed the establishment to report their encounter. The trio, along with Janson, rendezvoused with Antilles and Beruss before the Rogues entered their [[Starfighter/Legends|fighters]] to rescue Celchu and New Republic [[operative]] [[Winter Celchu|Winter]], both of whom had been captured prior and were attempting an escape in a [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|Y-wing]] on Axxila. Rogue Squadron entered a dogfight with the pirate [[Ugly/Legends|fighters]] tailing the Y-wing and emerged victorious. Escorting Celchu and Winter, Huwla and the pilots then landed in a [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]], where she complimented Ibtisam flying wing cover.[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 31|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 31]] The group was alerted to the presence of Imperial ships entering Axxila led by Admiral [[Delak Krennel]] and subsequently prepared to leave, during which Huwla offered to help the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] [[Chewbacca]] mount a [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam]] to his [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|starship]], the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']]. Fel, using the secrecy of his [[Desertion/Legends|defection]] from the Empire as a cover, contacted Krennel and issued him an [[AT3 Directive]], which compelled the admiral to withdraw his forces from the [[Axxila system/Legends|system]]. The action allowed Huwla and the group to depart Axxila safely. ===Rescuing the Grand Vizier=== {{Quote|I'm more banged up than my ship.''"
"''I enjoyed workout, too. You are good fighter.''"
"''Not so bad yourself, Xarcce.|Ilo and Huwla, prior to taking off from Ciutric IV|}} [[File:RetreatToCiutricIV-XWRS33.png|thumb|left|245px|Rogue Squadron retreats to Ciutric IV after being intercepted by Star Destroyers.]] Around [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]],''[[Star Wars Omnibus: X-Wing Rogue Squadron Volume 3]]'' states that the [[Star Wars: X-Wing Rogue Squadron: Mandatory Retirement|''Mandatory Retirement'']] story arc of ''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Rogue Squadron]]'' took place around a [[Standard year/Legends|year]] after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], which took place in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]] according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''. Therefore, ''Mandatory Retirement'' must take place around [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]]. Rogue Squadron was engaged in rest and recreation at a [[Unidentified location|location]]. Huwla visited a [[Unidentified gym|gym]], in which she changed the [[Gravity compensator/Legends|gravity compensator]] for a piece of weightlifting equipment to a setting of three. When Celchu later trained at the gym, he quickly gave up a weightlifting exercise when he attempted to operate the equipment due to the overbearing weight. Huwla and Ilo later had a sparring match, where they traded blows and knocked each other off balance. They were both rendered unconscious at the end of the fight. When Huwla later drank with Ilo at an establishment, Rogue Squadron was ordered by Admiral Ackbar to report for a mission briefing regarding Pestage's [[Evacuation of Ciutric|rescue]] from[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 32|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 32]] the world''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' [[Ciutric IV]]. Rogue Squadron—Huwla included—Aggressor Wing, and [[Commando Team One]] all sat in a briefing room while Antilles explained their mission; the Rogues and Aggressor Wing were to support Commando Team One while they rescued Pestage. Following the briefing, the units undertook an eighteen-[[Standard hour/Legends|hour]] journey to the [[Ciutric system/Legends|Ciutric system]] and [[Scout/Legends|scouted]] it before performing a [[Precision hyperspace jump|microjump]] to Ciutric IV. They subsequently engaged TIE fighters over the planet and enemy forces on the surface. After Commando Team One successfully extracted Pestage, they were prepared to leave the [[Star system/Legends|system]] when the [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] [[Reckoning (Imperial II-class)|''Reckoning'']] and [[Interdictor-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Interdictor]] ''[[Binder]]'' intercepted them, cutting off their escape route and forcing them to retreat to Ciutric IV's surface to stay the [[Local day/Legends|night]] while Aggressor Wing separated from them and fled.[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 33|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 33]] Later during the [[Standard day/Legends|day]], with the help of the [[Commando/Legends|commandos]] and a number of Rogues, Antilles sent out a call for reinforcements via the [[HoloNet/Legends|HoloNet]] before the squadron intercepted a group of TIE fighters in their X-wings. However, Ibtisam was shot down and killed. Huwla and the others watched Vakil mourn over her body when it was later brought to their place of stay by a rescue team.[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 34|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 34]] Rogue Squadron subsequently attacked the two Star Destroyers and TIE fighters above Ciutric IV, with Huwla operating under the [[Callsign/Legends|call sign]] [[Rogue Four/Legends|Rogue Four]]. They were soon reinforced by the [[Baudo-class star yacht|starship]] ''[[Pulsar Skate]]'' and[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 35|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 35]] its captain,{{CCG|set=Reflections II: Expanding the Galaxy|cardname=Mirax Terrik}} [[Mirax Terrik Horn|Mirax Terrik]], as well as Salm and Aggressor Wing, who were waiting at the edge of the system. Thanks to their intervention, the Imperial forces in the area temporarily retreated before the New Republic pilots returned to Ciutric IV's surface. Both squadrons then commenced a direct assault on the ''Reckoning'' and ''Binder''. The Rogues covered Aggressor Wing's Y-wings as they attacked the ''Reckoning''. When the ''Binder''{{'s}} [[Gravity well projector/Legends|artificial gravity generators]] were destroyed after the ship suffered serious damage, Huwla reported the fact to the rest of the pilots while she fired upon a TIE interceptor. Both Star Destroyers fled the system, allowing Commando Team One to leave Ciutric IV's surface. However, Pestage's rescue ended in failure when the [[Advisor/Legends|advisor]] abandoned his rescue team, stayed behind on the planet, and leaked the New Republic's plans to Krennel, who proceeded to murder him and seize his personal territory. Afterward, Huwla was present at a [[Funeral of Ibtisam|funeral]] for Ibtisam as one of the Rogues standing over her coffin. By [[6 ABY/Legends|6 ABY]],''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' places the events of ''[[X-Wing: Rogue Squadron]]'' in [[6 ABY/Legends|6 ABY]]. the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Bror Jace]] operated as Rogue Four.''[[X-Wing: Rogue Squadron]]'' ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|Before I learned frustration, I learned patience.|Xarcce Huwla|}} [[File:WhoNeedsPlourr-XW30.png|thumb|right|300px|Huwla (right) was friendly with Nrin Vakil (left) and Ibtisam (center).]] Xarcce Huwla had [[Color/Legends|black]] [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] and yellow skin. She had learned patience before learning frustration, stating the fact after Wedge Antilles apologized to her and other recruits for keeping them waiting at Base Delta-Nine. Despite volunteering to join Rogue Squadron and later feeling honored to be a squadron member, Huwla requested a transfer out of the squadron to Antilles. While she never avoided duty, she spoke logically to him regarding Rogue Squadron's high death count. By the end of the Brentaal IV campaign, however, Huwla had made friends with the squadron and never spoke of transfers again. She believed that while Tycho Celchu spoke of fear for relief, Plourr Ilo was a fool as she spoke against fear to hide it and did not wish to understand a lethal enemy like Baron Soontir Fel. However, when Derek Klivian mentioned the fact that he and his colleagues could never defeat Fel in a [[Flight simulator/Legends|flight simulation]] during their time at the [[Imperial Academy/Legends|Imperial Academy]], Huwla, struck with fear like the other Rogues around her, brought up her transfer to Antilles again. As a Rogue, Huwla developed friendly relations with Nrin Vakil and Ibtisam in the unit and complimented her fellow Rogues' good actions; she complimented Ibtisam's wing cover during a mission and enjoyed her workout with Ilo, calling her a "good fighter." ==Skills and abilities== {{Quote|You fly wing cover well, Ibtisam.''"
"''You're hard to keep up with, Xarcce.|Huwla and Ibtisam|}} As a starfighter pilot, Huwla was skilled enough to shoot down Imperial craft, racking up impressive kills against Fel's legendary 181st Imperial Fighter Wing during the Battle of Brentaal IV and cover her allies in the process. According to Ibtisam, she was "hard to keep up with" in flight. Aside from piloting, she could identify a merchant's cup as a fake by examining its make. Huwla was also skilled in hand-to-hand combat and could take on multiple opponents with little to no challenge. Vakil considered her a strong individual and informed Ilo about the fight in Axxila, which she called Huwla "tough" for when the two engaged in a sparring match that rendered both of them unconscious by the end. ==Equipment== [[File:XarrceNeutralizesTarget-XWRS21.png|thumb|left|300px|As a Rogue, Huwla flew an X-wing.]] As a Rogue Squadron pilot, Huwla flew a T-65 X-wing and wore an orange [[Flight suit/Legends|flight suit]] under a white [[Flak vest/Legends|flak vest]] and a [[Flight helmet/Legends|flight helmet]] emblazoned with the [[Alliance Starbird]]. When not flying, she wore a red [[Clothing/Legends|vest]] accented with gold, light brown pants, brown [[Boot/Legends|boots]], and a gold covering wrapped around her left [[Limb/Legends|arm]], and occasionally a [[Bracelet/Legends|bracelet]] on the other arm. Following the Brentaal IV campaign, while aboard ''Home One'', she wore a light brown outfit under a red vest. While on Axxila, Huwla donned multiple sets of clothing at different occasions; a green [[shirt]] and brown pants when meeting the other Rogues at a building, green pants and a green shirt at an establishment prior to combatting pirates, and a green crop top and green shorts at a hangar following a dogfight with pirates. When sparring with Ilo, she wore a teal-colored outfit that included a headgear, a vest, [[Glove/Legends|gloves]], and pads for the arms, elbows, knees, shins, and feet. The pads were secured onto the body with a gray material. At two separate funerals, Huwla wore a [[New Republic officers' dress uniform]]. ==Behind the scenes== [[File:Xwing21.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Xarcce Huwla debuted in ''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 21.]] Xarcce Huwla was created for [[Dark Horse Comics|Dark Horse's]] ''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Rogue Squadron]]'' [[Star Wars comic books|comic-book]] series, first appearing in the [[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 21|twenty-first issue]] published on [[August 6]], [[1997]].{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54666/star_wars_x-wing_rogue_squadron_1995_21|text=Star Wars: X-Wing Rogue Squadron (1995) #21}} The issue was written by [[Michael A. Stackpole]] and illustrated by [[John Nadeau]]. The [[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 28|twenty-eighth issue]] misspells the character's first name as "Xarcee," while the article "[[Who's Who in Rogue Squadron]]," written by [[Jason Fry]] and published in the ''[[Star Wars Insider]]'' magazine's [[Star Wars Insider 59|fifty-ninth issue]], [[Wizards of the Coast]]'s [[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]] supplement ''[[Ultimate Alien Anthology]]'', [[Peet Janes]]' [[Star Wars Handbook 1: X-Wing Rogue Squadron|''Star Wars Handbook'' 1: ''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'']]—a companion piece for the ''Rogue Squadron'' comic series—[[Bill Slavicsek]]'s [[2000]] reference book ''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'', and [[2008]]'s ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' spell it as "Xarrce." This article follows the spelling provided with Huwla's first appearance. Huwla's left arm covering's length is inconsistently depicted between panels of the [[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 23|twenty-third issue]] of ''Rogue Squadron''. Huwla's skin color is also incorrectly depicted as red in the [[X-Wing: Rogue Leader 3|third issue]] of the [[Star Wars: X-Wing: Rogue Leader|''X-Wing: Rogue Leader'']] comic series and gray in the [[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 30|thirthieth issue]] of ''Rogue Squadron''. ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Xarcce Huwla}} *[[X-Wing: Rogue Leader 3|''X-Wing: Rogue Leader'' 3]] *[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 21|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 21]] {{1st}} *[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 22|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 22]] *[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 23|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 23]] *[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 24|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 24]] *[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 26|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 26]] *[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 28|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 28]] {{ID|Xarcee}} *[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 29|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 29]] *[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 30|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 30]] *[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 31|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 31]] *[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 32|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 32]] *[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 33|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 33]] *[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 34|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 34]] *[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 35|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 35]] ==Sources== *[[Star Wars Handbook 1: X-Wing Rogue Squadron|''Star Wars Handbook'' 1: ''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'']] {{ID|Xarrce}} *''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' {{ID|Xarrce}} *{{InsiderCite|59|Who's Who in Rogue Squadron}} {{ID|Xarrce}} *{{FactFile|14|ROG 1-2|X-Wing Rogue Squadron}} {{Po}} *{{FactFile|61|TIE 7-8|TIE Interceptor}} {{C|Starfighter only}} *''[[Ultimate Alien Anthology]]'' {{ID|Xarrce}} *{{FactFile|130|RIT 5-6|Galactic Rituals}} {{Po}} *''[[Star Wars: The Comics Companion]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' {{ID|Xarrce}} {{StartBox}} {{SuccessionBox |title=[[Rogue Four/Legends|Rogue Four]] |years=c.
